differences.title: 1. RENDERING LOCATION
differences.leftDesc: Rendered on the server, reduces client bundle size and improves initial load.
differences.rightDesc: Rendered in the browser, interactive by default but can increase bundle size.

differences.title: 2. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Can access databases and file systems directly on the server.
differences.rightDesc: Fetches data via APIs, ensuring separation and security.

differences.title: 3. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: No JavaScript sent to the client for initial rendering.
differences.rightDesc: Requires JavaScript to be sent to the client for rendering and interactivity.

differences.title: 4. INTERACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Not inherently interactive; requires passing props or using specific patterns.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for rich user interactions and dynamic UIs.

differences.title: 5. SEO
differences.leftDesc: Content is fully available in the initial HTML response for crawlers.
differences.rightDesc: Requires JavaScript execution by crawlers, which is improving but not always perfect.

differences.title: 6. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State managed on the server, simplifying client logic.
differences.rightDesc: Manages UI state in the browser, enabling complex client-side interactivity.

differences.title: 7. INITIAL LOAD PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Quicker initial paint as less JavaScript needs to be downloaded and parsed.
differences.rightDesc: Can be slower if large JavaScript bundles need to be downloaded and executed.
